transcript.clean_text: the general Italia is available to watch live and On Demand every day on gcn plus and on stage 4 of the race the Peloton were about to take on their most difficult challenge in terms of Parkour so far in this year's 106th Edition the 176 ride is still in the race lining up knowing they'd have to take on almost 4 000 meters of climbing up and down all day and a possibility for a real challenge to the mayanoza or would the lead as they began leaving venezer heading to Lago lachino the scene of a victory for Domenico pod surivo the last time the race visited in 2012. three categorized climbs the hardest climbs of the race so far second category climbs with kolemolela the final of those three climbs for lots of uncategorized Rises and Falls a difficult day and the weather not playing ball either rainy cloudy and pretty darn miserable with 175 kilometers to take on as soon as the flag was waived it would be attack followed by attack followed by yes you guessed it more attacks 80 kilometers it would take for a breakaway to form some dangerous Riders got up the road others lost contact and there were worries for teams like UA Emirates who had their leader in a split draw while maida he needed to be towed back he would come pink jersey with Nepal was dressed up was wrapped up and Ben Healy and Calum scottson were all Riders among many who tried to continue to attack it'll be over the top of one of the climbs that the first move would start to go and when it did form the day's break consisted of seven Riders only one of them having won a Grand Tour stage previously that man the Frenchman Warren bargill up there on the break with him Andres lechnitzen of Norway the man from the Arctic Circle there at 23 years of age the youngest and also the best placed in the general classification he would stand to take the pink jersey if they were all to get there together they looked increasingly like too that Remco ivnopol was ready to give it up took them on to the final climb that after Lexington had picked up three bonus seconds at the final intermediate Sprint independent things were about to get nervous all picking up positions for the final climbank following on conchi launching the first attack also in the break screensaver trying their best to take the stage win for Trek sing alfredo would end up isolated inside the final few kilometers Elon Von Wilder the last of his teammates to be dropped lion Supply is controlling the GC group all the while the fight for the stage win was going on further up the mountain rejoining Lexington after he'd gone on the attack three kilometers to go at this stage and when they got into the final kilometer it will be who would outsprint Lexington a 27 year old from the French Alps winning in the rugged Hills of southern Italy legnes in second watching the time Gap and when it ticked over a minute and a half he would move into the Mayan also Delight for Azure desert who get their aim of a stage when the third in the GC the man finishing behind him disappointed as he crossed the line but that disappointment would soon turn to Joy the mayadosa and the race lead on his back that's because the bunch came in at just over two minutes down amongst the rest of the GC contenders no real change emotions strong and visible for lexness and disappointed at missing out on the stage after suffering some difficult times off the bike recently he'd be finishing just behind Barry Banker but on the podium smiling and delighted Dear Santa in the race lead Azure does cell taking the stage the difference now in the GC 28 seconds between Lexus and IV Nepal and lexness and two taking the white juice you mean the eve Nepal could have a night off all of the podium shenanigans stage five is an interesting one one that could end up in some sort of a bunch Sprint but there's plenty of difficult terrain that will give anybody wishing to get in a breakaway a chance that they need to try and take it home all the way Salerno is the destination 171 kilometers on the menu again well over two and a half thousand kilometers of climbing but a flatish finish promises to be an interesting start once again a reminder you can watch the jittery tally at live and On Demand on gcn Plus foreign
